metadata.notes: Generated by Protocol Intelligence Platform. SR- IDs are placeholders.

general_information.notes: To be completed by Risk Manager upon IRMS entry

study_overview.title: AN INTERVENTIONAL EFFICACY AND SAFETY, PHASE 3, DOUBLE-BLIND, 2-ARM STUDY TO INVESTIGATE ORALLY ADMINISTERED IBUZATRELVIR COMPARED WITH PLACEBO IN NON-HOSPITALIZED SYMPTOMATIC ADULT AND ADOLESCENT PARTICIPANTS WITH COVID-19 WHO ARE AT HIGH RISK OF PROGRESSING TO SEVERE ILLNESS

critical_factors.critical_data: Primary endpoint data (COVID-19 related ED visits, hospitalization, death); Secondary endpoint data (time to symptom resolution, CRPE, PACE, Long COVID/PASC PROs, SARS-CoV-2 RNA levels); PK plasma data.
critical_factors.critical_process: Accurate capture and adjudication of hospitalization/ED visit records; Consistent participant training and compliance for daily symptom diaries and Long COVID PROs; Proper collection, processing, and shipment of NP/nasal swabs for viral RNA assessment.

critical_data.signal: Primary Endpoint — COVID-19 related ED visits, hospitalization, or death
Secondary Endpoints — Symptom resolution, Long COVID PROs, SARS-CoV-2 RNA

critical_process.signal: ePRO symptom diaries and PRO assessments
NP/nasal swab collection for biomarker analysis

critical_factors.critical_data: TEAEs, SAEs, AEs leading to discontinuation; Prespecified cardiovascular, renal, and pulmonary events (CRPE); Safety lab data (AST, ALT, eGFR).
critical_factors.critical_process: Timely reporting and differentiation of COVID-19 disease progression versus true adverse events; Monitoring of liver and renal function labs; Routine contraception checks and pregnancy testing for WOCBP.

critical_data.signal: Exclusion criteria for active liver disease and severe renal impairment

critical_process.signal: Inclusion criteria — WOCBP highly effective contraception requirement

critical_factors.factor_name: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results
critical_factors.critical_data: ePRO data (symptom diaries, Long COVID/PASC); Blinding data; Concomitant medication logs.
critical_factors.critical_process: Retaining the blind between Ibuzatrelvir and Placebo arms; ePRO device setup, data collection compliance, and data transmission; Ensuring completion of remote telephone follow-up visits (Weeks 8, 16, 20).

critical_factors.critical_data: Age and risk factor matrix data; RAT results and dates; Symptom onset dates; eGFR and liver function lab values.
critical_factors.critical_process: Verification of confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection within 1 day prior to randomization; Confirmation of symptom onset within 5 days; Strict adherence to the complex age-based risk factor requirements (e.g., 12-49 years needing moderate immunocompromise).

critical_data.signal: Inclusion 4 — RAT within 1 day, symptom onset within 5 days
Inclusion 3 — Complex age and risk factor matrix

critical_process.signal: Enrollment caps (20%) for specific age groups (12-49 and 50-64)

critical_factors.critical_data: Dosing logs and IP accountability records.
critical_factors.critical_process: IP accountability for outpatient administration; Ensuring participants take exactly 600 mg (or matching placebo) BID for 5 days (10 doses total); Verification that participants are unable or unwilling to take standard-of-care nirmatrelvir/ritonavir.

critical_data.signal: 600 mg orally every 12 hours for 5 days (10 doses)

risks.risk_description: Enrolled participant(s) do not meet the complex age-based risk factor matrix or timing requirements for COVID-19 symptom onset.
risks.additional_context: The protocol requires symptom onset within 5 days and has a highly specific age-to-risk-factor matrix (e.g., 12-49 years must have moderate immunocompromise, while 75+ need no risk factors). There are also specific enrollment caps (20%) for certain age groups.

associated_causes.cause: Miscalculation of the 5-day symptom onset window by site staff or participant recall bias.

usdm_trigger.signal: Initial onset of symptoms attributable to COVID-19 within 5 days prior to randomization

associated_causes.cause: Misclassification of risk factors or failure to meet the specific age-bracket requirements.

usdm_trigger.signal: Presence of risk factors for progression to severe COVID-19 at the time of screening based on age

controls.control_description: Programmed edit checks in EDC to validate the age-to-risk-factor matrix and symptom onset timing.
controls.additional_context: Ensures real-time validation of complex inclusion criteria 3 and 4.

controls.control_description: Routine medical review of enrolled participants to ensure adherence to the risk factor matrix and exclusion of severe renal/liver impairment.
controls.additional_context: Focuses on early detection of eligibility deviations.

risks.risk_description: Participants take prohibited concomitant medications or other COVID-19 antivirals during the study.
risks.additional_context: The study excludes participants who have received other antivirals (nirmatrelvir/ritonavir, remdesivir, molnupiravir) within 30 days and prohibits specific concomitant medications. Because this is an outpatient study, participants may seek external treatments if their symptoms worsen.

associated_causes.cause: Outpatient setting where participants may seek external treatment from their primary care physician for worsening COVID-19 symptoms.

usdm_trigger.signal: non-hospitalized symptomatic adult and adolescent participants

associated_causes.cause: Lack of participant understanding regarding prohibited medications and prior antiviral washout periods.

usdm_trigger.signal: Current use of any prohibited concomitant medication(s) / Has received any other antiviral

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

controls.control_description: Routine review of concomitant medication logs and protocol deviations related to prohibited medications.
controls.additional_context: Monitors for use of rescue medications or prohibited antivirals.

risks.risk_description: NP/nasal swab samples for SARS-CoV-2 RNA and plasma samples for PK are incorrectly obtained, stored, or shipped.
risks.additional_context: The study relies heavily on NP/nasal samples to assess virologic rebound and viral RNA <LLOQ (Secondary Objectives), as well as plasma PK sampling. These require strict handling procedures at outpatient clinics to maintain sample integrity.

associated_causes.cause: Site staff error in NP swab collection technique leading to insufficient viral RNA yield.

usdm_trigger.signal: Change from baseline in SARS-CoV-2 RNA in NP/nasal samples over time

associated_causes.cause: Temperature excursions or delays during shipment of PK plasma samples.

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

controls.control_description: Provide detailed laboratory manual and training to sites on proper NP/nasal swab collection and PK plasma processing/shipping.
controls.additional_context: Critical for ensuring virologic and PK endpoints are evaluable.

risks.risk_description: Trial conduct is adversely impacted by participant failure to complete daily symptom diaries or attend long-term follow-up visits.
risks.additional_context: The study requires daily symptom tracking for 28 days and long-term follow-up up to Week 24 (including telephone calls at Weeks 8, 16, 20) to assess Long COVID/PASC. There is a high risk of attrition or ePRO non-compliance once acute COVID-19 symptoms resolve.

associated_causes.cause: Participant fatigue with daily ePRO symptom diaries over a 28-day period.

usdm_trigger.signal: Time (days) to sustained resolution of all targeted COVID-19 symptoms through Day 28

associated_causes.cause: Loss to follow-up after acute COVID-19 recovery, missing critical Week 24 Long COVID assessments.

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

usdm_drivers.signal: Long COVID/PASC patient-reported outcomes (PRO) at Week 24

controls.control_type: Routine Review and Remediation of metrics for loss to follow up (QTL/KRI)
controls.control_description: Monitor ePRO compliance rates and missed visit rates (especially telephone follow-ups) via centralized dashboards.
controls.additional_context: Allows early intervention for participants showing signs of fatigue or disengagement.

controls.control_description: Implement participant retention strategies and reminders for long-term follow-up visits.
controls.additional_context: Crucial for capturing the Week 24 Long COVID/PASC endpoints.

risks.risk_description: Reliability of trial results is impacted by unblinding or poor ePRO data collection for subjective symptom endpoints.
risks.additional_context: This is a double-blind, placebo-controlled trial where subjective symptom resolution and Long COVID PROs are key secondary endpoints. Maintaining the blind and ensuring robust ePRO data integrity are critical to the study's statistical validity.

associated_causes.cause: Accidental unblinding due to distinct IP characteristics or specific adverse event profiles associated with Ibuzatrelvir.
ePRO system transmission failures or device setup errors preventing the capture of daily symptom data.

critical_factors_linked: Data Integrity and Reliability of Trial Results

usdm_drivers.signal: Subjective PRO endpoints (symptom resolution, Long COVID)

controls.control_type: Third Party management (i.e., assessment, contracting, onboarding, oversight)
controls.control_description: Strict oversight of the eCOA/ePRO vendor to ensure device reliability, timely data transmission, and helpdesk support for participants.
controls.additional_context: Mitigates the risk of missing data for key secondary endpoints.
